skill.name: write-a-skill
skill.description: Create new agent skills with clear structure, progressive disclosure, examples, references, and optional utility scripts.
skill.summary: Guide agents through creating well structured skills with useful triggers, examples, and supporting resources.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backtick fences as Ruby shell execution and ordinary description text as weak cryptography. Review found no executable code, no network access, no credential access, and no prompt injection attempt. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from its optional guidance about creating future utility scripts.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static findings at the listed lines are Markdown code fences and example blocks. They are documentation syntax, not Ruby shell backtick execution, and no command is executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged characters delimit Markdown examples in SKILL.md. There is no Ruby file, script entry point, command string, or execution context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: Static weak cryptography alerts occur on prose lines about descriptions and checklists. Review found no hashing, encryption, checksum, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain natural-language uses of description-related words. No code or configuration implements a weak cryptographic algorithm.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable guidance for building useful AI skills. This skill helps create structured skill files with clear triggers, examples, references, and review steps.
actual_capabilities: Defines a process for gathering skill requirements before drafting.
Shows the expected structure for SKILL.md and optional supporting files.
Provides a reusable SKILL.md template with frontmatter and sections.
Explains how to write descriptions that trigger the right skill.
Gives criteria for adding scripts and splitting reference files.
Includes a review checklist for consistency and maintainability.
limitations: It does not generate a complete skill without user requirements.
It does not validate final files with an automated schema checker.
It does not provide domain-specific examples beyond general skill structure.
It does not include executable helper scripts.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Turn a recurring team workflow into a structured skill with triggers, steps, and examples.

use_cases.title: Improve Skill Documentation
use_cases.description: Refine a draft SKILL.md so agents can load it only when it is relevant.

use_cases.title: Package Reusable Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Decide when to add reference files, examples, and scripts for a repeatable agent task.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a skill for [task]. Ask me for the requirements, then draft the SKILL.md structure.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Skill Triggers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill idea and improve the description so an agent knows exactly when to load it.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Supporting Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether this skill needs reference files, examples, or scripts. Explain what belongs in each file.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Draft Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this draft skill for structure, progressive disclosure, trigger quality, examples, and maintainability.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for reviewing release notes.
output_examples.output: A requirements checklist, a proposed skill description, and a draft section outline for release note review.
A clearer description with specific trigger phrases, scope boundaries, and suggested wording.
A decision explaining whether deterministic validation or formatting would benefit from a helper script.

best_practices: Start with the task, trigger conditions, and expected outputs before writing sections.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move rarely used details into reference files.
Review descriptions for clear trigger phrases and consistent terminology.
anti_patterns: Writing a broad description that could match many unrelated user requests.
Putting long reference material directly into SKILL.md.
Adding scripts when plain instructions would be simpler and safer.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create agent skills with clear instructions, triggers, examples, and optional supporting resources.

faq.question: Does it create executable scripts?
faq.answer: It can recommend when scripts are useful, but it does not include any script file itself.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review a draft for structure, description quality, file splitting, and examples.

faq.question: Which tools can use the skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it domain specific?
faq.answer: No. It provides a general process for creating skills in many domains.

faq.question: Why were security alerts dismissed?
faq.answer: The alerts matched Markdown fences and prose. Review found no executable code or cryptographic implementation.
